title: Three-way XOR involving bit 31 of a vector evaluates incorrectly
body:
Good day!

I ran into an issue where Verilator seems to evaluate a three-way single-bit XOR expression incorrectly. I have seen #4709 which could be related, but it is not obvious to me that this is the same problem.

Assumed x[38] and x[31] are well defined ('0' or '1'), the verilog expression below should be always false, because A xor B xor A = B, so left-hand-side and right-hand-side of the compare operator are equal. 

However, Verilator evaluates this to true:

```
wire bad    = {x[38:32], x[38] ^ x[31] ^ x[38]} != x[38:31];
```
I prepared a pretty minimal example (derived from the provided Verilator example in examples/make_tracing.c). The problem seems to be specific to bit index 31, and to the order of the three XOR operands. I declared some similar `good` wires that all evaluate as expected.

To reproduce, clone my repo https://github.com/maartenboersma/verilator-xor and run `make`:
```
$ git clone https://github.com/maartenboersma/verilator-xor.git
$ make
```

I am on Linux, x86, Ubuntu 22.04 LTS. I tried verilator version 5.012/5.020/5.024/5.026 and master (as of today=`607c19a67`).
